name: Thelma Carpenter
abstract: Thelma Carpenter (January 15, 1922 – May 14, 1997) was an American jazz singer and actress, best known as "Miss One", the Good Witch of the North in the movie The Wiz.

concerts.label: Benefit: Common Council for American Unity
Midnight Performance: Pittsburgh Courier Poll Winners Jazz Concert
The All-Star New Year's Eve Revue
The All-Star New Year's Eve Revue
Newport Jazz Festival: Schlitz Salute to Café Society

works.t: My Kind of Boy Is You
If I Could Be with You (One Hour Tonight)

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: The mention of Thelma Carpenter is made solely by the [INTERVIEWER] ('I knew another little gal, Thelma Carpenter'). The [INTERVIEWEE] does not respond to this specific mention or provide any personal connection.

about_quotes.speaker: David Albert Francis
about_quotes.evidence: At that time, the union was still trying to get clubs to join, so this was a non-union club, the Rosebud, which was run by the uncle of Thelma Carpenter. I remembered Thelma when she was like 11 or 12 years old

about_quotes.speaker: Bud Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: Playing for people like the DeMarco Sisters, there was a well-known, the Carpenter girl, I forget her first name -- Thelma Carpenter, played -- did a lot of dates

about_quotes.speaker: Etta Jones
about_quotes.evidence: And then when I was at the Onyx Club on 52nd Street... When I met Thelma I asked her, “Do you hear any of you in me?” And she said, “Maybe.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Etta Jones interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 2, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Jo Jones
about_quotes.evidence: We got Thelma Carpenter. ... We had Thelma to come in ... We rehearsed her and showed her things and put on the gowns

about_quotes.speaker: Doc Cheatham
about_quotes.evidence: No, Thelma was with Coleman Hawkins band, at that time. But we were at the Golden Gate together, both bands.
